<par>The Sani forms <latinform>tɕhe³³tʂɒ³³</latinform>, <latinform>tɕhe⁴⁴tʂɑ³³</latinform>, <latinform>tɕʰe³³ tʂɑ³³</latinform> are puzzling, since either syllable is a plausible reflex of this root. Perhaps two Sani variants have developed, one (with aspirated initial) < <reconstruction>*kyak</reconstruction>, meaning 'navel'; and the other (with unaspirated initial) < <reconstruction>*gyak</reconstruction>, meaning 'cord'.</par>
